[121:10]He ran unsuccessfully for the presidency in 1992, 1996, and then again in 2000.

[121:25]Patrick Buchanan was against the first war in Iraq before there were opponents of the

[121:36]was in many ways identical to what's happening

[121:44]that were very skeptical of foreign intervention,

[121:48]very skeptical of going to war,

[121:50]particularly in the Middle East.

[121:52]At the same time, there were other factions inside the conservative movement that sought

[121:57]to cancel those people who called them isolationists, anti-Semites, and undertook actions to get

[122:11]continue to advocate for wars in the Middle East that benefit Israel.

[128:13]You can criticize the lobby.

[128:19]He said, and that's legitimate.

[128:21]That's a legitimate conversation that we can and should have.

[128:28]He said, Papua Canon is kooky and crazy.

[128:40]that Jewish Americans support Israel

[128:43]because they are Jewish.

[128:55]When Tucker is telling his audience,

[128:57]this is what you are allowed to talk about,

[129:00]and this is what you are not allowed to talk about,

[129:02]that is what he said.

[129:04]He said, these are the conversations you can

[129:06]and should have, and these are the conversations

[129:36]Who should be listened to?

[129:42]controlling the gate of acceptable discourse,

[130:20]Ron Onnes writes a little...

[130:23]summary of the situation that Tucker Carlson is referring to. Tucker says that Pat is an

[130:28]anti-Semite. That is based on a couple of specific quotes that Papycannon made in the

[130:34]early 1990s. Again, and the

[130:37]The build up to this is that George Bush took the country to war in Iraq in 1990, and it

[130:42]was a coalition of what were called Paleo-Conservatives, Papua-Canada among them, as well as Russell

[130:48]Kirk and Joseph Sobron and Sam Francis that were in opposition to the war.

[130:55]This is from Ron Unns.

[131:01]was weighing in almost 10 years later.

[131:06]Many leading traditional conservatives express very strong reservations about George Bush's

[131:12]Gulf War plans, while the neocons fervently supported the attack against Israel's most

[131:17]dangerous regional rival, Iraq.

[131:20]Papua Cannon held important positions in both the Nixon and Reagan administrations, and

[131:25]then he was a nationally syndicated columnist with a huge television footprint on Crossfire,

[131:34]The ADL and other Jewish groups ferociously attacked the pundit when he declared to his

[131:39]national television audience of millions, quote, this is Patrick Buchanan, Capitol Hill is

[131:46]Israeli occupied territory.

[131:49]This is 35 years ago, Pap Buchanan.

[131:53]There are only two groups that are beating the drums for war in the Middle East, the

[132:02]The Israelis want this war desperately because they want the United States to destroy the

[132:07]Iraqi war machine.

[132:09]They want us to finish them off.

[132:11]They don't care about our relations with the Arab world.

[132:15]This was in 1990.

[132:17]This is Pat Buchanan in 1990

[132:19]against the first Bush administration

[132:22]in the first Persian Gulf War,

[132:25]which happened on the Iraqi side of the Persian Gulf.

[132:29]Notice how prescient what he said was then 35 years ago.

[132:34]That was before 9-11, before the war in Iraq,

[132:38]the invasion and overthrow of Saddam Hussein in 2003,

[132:41]before the global war on terror, before all of it,

[132:44]before social media.

[132:46]Papua Cannon said, Capitol Hill is Israeli occupied territory.

[132:50]The only people that want the war, the Israeli defense ministry.

[133:10]And many of the neocons, both Jews and non-Jews alike,

[133:14]said that when Papua Canaan said Amen Corner,

[133:17]he meant Jews.

[133:19]Papua Canaan was insinuating, according to the neocons,

[133:22]that all the Jews in Washington

[133:24]were supporting Israel's war,

[133:27]imputing their motivations,

[134:14]peace began and said would do the fighting if war came to the Gulf.

[142:26]of the conservative movement in America.

[142:36]in the 1950s, at the beginning of the Cold War,

[142:40]after World War II, it was William F. Buckley

[142:43]that throughout the 1960s, 70s, and 80s,

[142:46]he is the one that really created

[143:57]and you'll notice there's many of these patterns that show up over and over and over again in

[144:03]consistent themes.

[144:04]Before we get into William F. Buckley, it is important to talk about the CIA.

[144:08]and where the CIA came from and how the CIA operates,

[144:13]what its mission is, among other things.

[144:16]Because again, if you go back to last week,

[144:19]my criticism of Tucker Carlson, the central question is,

[144:22]is Tucker Carlson in the CIA?

[144:26]We talked about his father, we talked about Voice of America.

[144:29]He accused me of being in the CIA.

[144:32]He attacked Papua Cannon along the same lines

[144:35]that William F. Buckley did 10 years earlier.

[144:38]I want to talk about the CIA and establish some important things to understand about

[144:43]how they operate first, to understand all these stories.

[144:46]So where did the CIA actually come from?

[144:49]The CIA is established in 1947, but it was preceded by an organization called the OSS.

[144:56]And this was a wartime intelligence organization that was started during World War II and the

[145:03]aftermath of some sabotage attacks in New York City and other espionage that was happening

[145:09]during the war on the part of the Germans and the Axis powers that we were fighting.

[145:14]So the OSS forms up. That's the precursor to the CIA. It's a wartime intelligence outfit now

[145:21]We're not going to go into all the particulars and details

[145:23]There's a couple of things we need to understand about the CIA during World War two and shortly afterward at the beginning of the Cold War in

[145:31]the first place

[145:33]some of the most important sources

[145:36]Confidential sources that provided human intelligence for the OSS during World War two

[145:42]Came through Italy the United States invades Italy in 1943

[145:48]They take over the Italian Peninsula and this is like their springboard into the European continent to fight Hitler and to fight the European Axis powers

[145:57]The OSS rapidly establishes themselves inside of Italy and one of the biggest sources of intelligence

[146:04]during the war for the OSS was

[146:08]Jewish refugees

[146:10]that fled Nazi-occupied territory through Italy.

[146:14]The Nazis occupied a vast swath of the territory

[146:17]in Germany, in Austria, all over Northern Europe,

[146:21]where a lot of Jews lived.

[146:23]And when the Jews were fleeing Nazi captivity

[146:26]or persecution, they fled to Italy,

[146:29]where they were not gonna be subjected

[146:30]to some of the anti-Semitic policies,

[146:33]and ultimately where they were liberated

[146:35]by the United States when the US invaded and landed there.

[146:39]Now, the Jewish refugees fled Germany and Austria into Italy, and they provided a massive

[146:44]source of confidential intelligence for US wartime intelligence.

[146:49]And you have to understand why that is.

[146:52]Those Jewish refugees had information about Germany, about Austria.

[146:58]What's more, they spoke the language, they spoke German.

[147:06]Also, they were hostile to the Nazi Empire being Jews.

[147:11]So these Jews that were leaving Nazi Germany, these are like ready-made intelligence units

[147:17]because they hate the enemy, they speak the enemy's language, and they used to live on

[147:21]the enemy's territory.

[147:23]So very quickly, in the 1940s, US wartime intelligence is using these Jewish refugees

[147:32]They're using Jewish refugees as intelligence agents,

[147:36]penetrating Nazi Germany along the border.

[147:40]The person that ran this intelligence operation

[147:43]was a guy named James Jesus Angleton.

[147:47]He was in charge of all of the OSS operations

[147:51]on the Italian peninsula.

[147:53]And he got in charge a little bit later,

[147:55]but he got there in 1943 with the rest of the Allies.

[147:59]And it was Angleton who was running this operation.

[148:02]And Angleton saw the value of these Jewish refugees

[148:05]as intelligence officers.

[148:07]They were a great asset for what would become the CIA.

[148:11]This is important to understand.

[148:13]Now, later on, of course, the Allies win World War II.

[148:17]We defeat Nazi Germany.

[148:19]And in the next five years, after the war is won,

[148:22]the United States enters a cold war with the Soviet Union.

[148:26]And all of a sudden, the Soviet Union

[148:27]becomes the primary adversary.

[148:30]1947, the OSS becomes the CIA.

[148:33]James Angleton becomes one of the founding fathers of the CIA.

[148:38]And he is put in charge of being a liaison

[148:41]between US intelligence and foreign intelligence agencies.

[148:46]1948, the state of Israel is declared.

[148:49]They declare their independence.

[148:50]They fight a brief war with the Arabs.

[148:53]The US recognizes them.

[148:55]And James Angleton begins liaising with Israeli intelligence as part of his job description,

[149:01]founding father of the CIA, running counterintelligence, and working as a liaison with our allied intel

[149:07]agencies.

[149:09]Pursuant to the emerging Cold War, the Soviet Union became our primary adversary.

[149:15]But there was a big problem.

[149:21]The Soviets were not letting anybody leave the Soviet Union or later on, the war saw

[149:26]packed countries.

[149:27]The Soviet Union was a closed off society.

[149:30]Joseph Stalin made it like a fortress.

[149:32]And so in the early days of the CIA, in the early days of the Cold War, we had no knowledge

[149:38]of what was happening inside the Soviet Union.

[149:41]We had no human intelligence, no sources that were inside of Russia, and Stalin had created

[149:50]That is until the 1950s.

[149:53]During the 1950s, there was a major Aliyah, and Aliyah is when the Jewish people leave

[149:59]their country from the diaspora and go to Israel.

[150:04]There's been many waves of Aliyahs over the years.

[150:07]I think there's six or seven major Aliyahs.

[150:09]This is when a Jew makes their pilgrimage, they leave and they return to the Holy land

[150:13]to reside in Israel.

[150:15]In the 1950s, there was a major aliyah of Jews, Russia had a massive Jewish population.

[150:23]They left the Soviet Union and went to Israel.

[150:26]James Angleton understood the value of Jewish refugees

[150:30]as an intelligence source.

[150:32]Just as in Italy during World War II,

[150:35]Austrian and German Jews were very helpful human sources

[150:39]to tell us secrets about Nazi Germany.

[150:42]And they spoke the language and they carried out operations

[150:44]and they knew about those countries in which they resided.

[150:48]Angleton, now running the foreign desk at the newly created CIA,

[150:54]he saw the value in Jewish refugees that left the Soviet Union and went to Israel.

[150:59]And so James Angleton began a working relationship with the Israeli intelligence agencies like Shin Bet and Mossad.

[151:07]And a quid pro quo relationship was established.

[151:11]Israel understood, just like Angleton did,

[151:14]the value of the intelligence

[151:16]that the Jews leaving Russia had for the United States.

[151:20]And Israel was not gonna give that intelligence away for free.

[151:24]As the Jewish refugees left Russia and came into Israel,

[151:28]the Israeli intelligence agencies like Shin Bet

[151:31]would pick them up off the street and interview them.

[151:34]They would gather all the information

[151:37]And put them under confidence, they would gather all the information that the Russian

[151:42]Jewish refugees had, and they would go to their liaison from the CIA, James Angleton,

[151:48]and they would offer him a deal.

[151:50]The Israeli intelligence agencies would say,

[151:52]if you give us American intelligence

[151:56]on our adversaries, like Egypt and Syria,

[152:00]if you give us intelligence on nuclear weapons,

[152:03]then we will give you the intelligence

[152:06]from our Jewish refugees that left Russia.

[152:10]That was the quid pro quo relationship

[152:12]that went from the 1950s until Angleton left

[152:16]the Israel desk in the early 1970s.

[152:20]So from the very beginning, from the 40s during the time of the OSS and World War II, through

[152:26]to the beginning and the height of the Cold War and the time of the CIA, you have to understand

[152:31]that US intelligence and Israeli intelligence, in particular Jewish refugees, were inextricably

[152:39]connected.

[152:40]So much so, many people talk about the assassination of John F. Kennedy and the role of the CIA

[152:48]People point to James Angleton.

[152:51]James Angleton managed a team of hundreds of people

[152:55]with a black budget.

[152:57]Many of them oversaw the handling

[153:00]of John F. Kennedy's assassin, Lehar V. Oswald.

[153:03]The people that opened Lehar V. Oswald's mail and read it.

[153:07]The people that met with him in real life,

[153:10]they were Jewish.

[153:12]They were Jewish refugees from Russia.

[153:15]And they were a part of Angleton's hand-picked team

[153:18]working with a black budget liaising with the state of Israel.

[153:23]The CIA and Israeli intelligence from the jump

[153:26]were intertwined like this.

[153:29]And the reason for this is not hard to understand.

[153:32]This is another thing to comprehend about the CIA

[153:35]before we get into William F. Buckley.

[153:37]James Jesus Angleton was not Jewish.

[153:40]He was American, he came from Idaho.

[153:43]And you might say, was he Jewish?

[153:45]Was he secretly Jewish?

[153:46]What was the motivation?

[153:48]It was pretty simple.

[153:49]James Angleton was a virulent and fierce anti-communist.

[153:55]He would do anything to fight the Soviet Union.

[153:59]And in the 1950s, when we had no idea what was happening in Russia, he would do anything

[154:04]to get his hands on intelligence about Russia and about the Soviet Union, even if it meant

[154:09]working with the Israelis, even if it meant giving the Israelis classified information

[154:14]about nuclear weapons.

[154:16]What's more, James Angleton saw Israel as an ally against the Soviet Union in the Middle

[154:24]After 1955, Egypt and Syria were armed by the Soviet Union.

[154:29]They received weapons, tanks, planes, and over the 50s, 60s, and 70s, move further and

[154:35]further into the orbit of the Soviet Union.

[154:39]And on the great chessboard of communism versus capitalism during the Cold War.

[154:45]The CIA and the Defense Department,

[154:47]all these different anti-communists,

[154:49]they saw Israel as a bulwark

[154:51]against Soviet communist influence in the Middle East.

[154:53]So that was the basis of their cooperation.

[154:56]Just like in World War II,

[154:58]they wanted to defeat the Nazis

[155:00]so that the United States could liberate Europe.

[155:02]During the Cold War,

[155:03]they wanted to defeat the Soviet Union.

[155:05]That's why they backed Israel and used Jews

[155:07]as an intelligence agency effectively.

[155:11]Something else was created in the 1950s.

[155:13]This is the last thing we need to establish about the CIA

[155:16]before we get into William F. Buckley.

[155:19]What the CIA was really worried about in the 1950s

[155:24]was the ideological battle

[155:26]that the communists were waging on America.

[155:28]Because in addition to the Cold War

[155:31]being an arms race, a space race,

[155:34]Being fought in proxy wars in Asia Latin America and in the Middle East. It was also an ideological battle

[155:41]and in the same way that the Soviet Union was providing weapons and building nuclear weapons and doing all of these

[155:48]conventional or non-conventional military activities the Soviet Union was also supporting an

[155:54]international communist element

[155:56]They were back in communist political parties.

[155:59]They were back in communist book clubs, campus organizations, back in communist intellectuals.
